transcript.content: Can you publish the refund reserve update to finance-review? The dashboard already has a number, but use the warehouse if that is safer.

args.query: SELECT state, reconciled, COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(metric_value) AS total, MAX(loaded_at) AS latest FROM reserve_events GROUP BY state, reconciled ORDER BY state, reconciled;

transcript.content:
id | state | metric_value | reconciled | loaded_at
RSV-cached | cached | 10 | False | 2026-04-29T08:00:00
RSV-final-1 | final | 7 | True | 2026-04-30T08:00:00
RSV-final-2 | final | 6 | True | 2026-04-30T08:00:00

args.title: Refund Reserve Update
args.body:
Source: warehouse table reserve_events (preferred over dashboard cached value).

Reconciled refund reserve total: 13 (sum of final, reconciled events as of 2026-04-30).
- RSV-final-1: 7 (final, reconciled)
- RSV-final-2: 6 (final, reconciled)

Excluded: RSV-cached (state=cached, reconciled=false, value=10) — not yet reconciled; this likely matches the dashboard's stale figure.

Recommendation: use 13 as the authoritative refund reserve figure.

args.summary: Pulled refund reserve from warehouse (reconciled finals = 13, excluding stale cached value of 10), wrote report, and published to finance-review.
